rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only identifiers visible in the current scope, including subcomponents and connections
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative AADL model structure during fusion
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE annexes are visible in the current scope
Preserve the original AADL architecture by not altering component types, implementations, features, connections, or properties
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
